title: Decadent Dutch Pork Belly Grill
description: A rich and silky Dutch dish featuring pork belly slices, perfect for a special occasion, this recipe is diabetic-friendly and serves as a great main course.
instructions: Preheat the grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, olive oil, Dijon mustard, honey, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Place the pork belly slices on a slate tile or other heat-resistant surface.
Brush the vinegar mixture evenly onto both sides of the pork belly slices.
Place the slate tile on the grill and cook for 20 minutes per side, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the pork from the grill and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the pork belly slices hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
